title: Manchester tart
subtitle: Jam, banana, and set custard in a shortcrust case
excerpt: A Northern English tart: a blind-baked shortcrust case filled with raspberry jam, sliced banana, set cornflour custard, and finished with desiccated coconut and glacé cherries. Served cold.
sourceNotes: Manchester tart is a Northern English dessert documented in school-dinner and domestic cookery from the early twentieth century. The combination of raspberry jam, banana, and vanilla custard in a pastry case was a staple of British institutional cooking. This version follows the traditional formula with a shortcrust pastry case and a cornflour-set custard.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the day before. Keeps refrigerated for 2 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf — for blind-baking the pastry case only; no further oven baking required

glossaryTerms.definition: A custard set with cornflour rather than egg yolks, made by whisking cornflour and sugar into cold milk, then cooking until the mixture thickens. It is more stable than egg custard and firms enough to slice when chilled. Traditional in school-dinner desserts.

content.text: Manchester tart is a cold dessert: a pastry case blind-baked and cooled, then filled with jam, banana, and a poured
that sets in the fridge. The custard must be poured while hot over the fruit — it will not flow once it begins to cool.

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} (for the pastry) into the {{plain-flour}}, {{icing-sugar}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs. Mix in the egg yolk and 2 tablespoons of cold water until the dough just comes together. Chill for 20 minutes. Roll out and line a 23 cm tart tin. Prick the base, line with baking paper, and fill with baking beans. Blind-bake at 190°C fan for 15 minutes. Remove the beans and paper and bake for a further 5–8 minutes until the pastry is dry and lightly golden. Cool completely.
Spread the {{raspberry-jam}} over the base of the cooled pastry case. Arrange the sliced {{bananas}} over the jam.

Whisk the {{cornflour}} and {{caster-sugar}} with 4 tablespoons of the {{whole-milk}} to a smooth paste. Bring the remaining milk to a simmer in a saucepan. Pour the hot milk onto the cornflour paste, whisking constantly, then return to the pan. Cook over medium heat, stirring continuously, for 3–4 minutes until thick. Remove from the heat and stir in the {{vanilla-extract}} and {{unsalted-butter}} (for the custard). Pour immediately over the banana in the pastry case.

Leave the tart to cool for 20 minutes, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours until the custard is fully set. Before serving, scatter the {{desiccated-coconut}} over the top and arrange the halved {{glace-cherries}} decoratively.
